caps.reason: You cannot be placed abroad without a passport, so your readiness is held at needs prep until you have one in hand. It is the first thing to sort, and SA passport turnaround is usually a few weeks.

reason.CA(SA) — SAICA: CA(SA) is the gold standard: SAICA's recognition agreements open UK, Australian, NZ, Canadian and Irish membership with no further exams.
reason.AGA(SA) / Accounting Technician — SAICA: AGA(SA) is recognised but does not carry full CA(SA) reciprocity, so expect a destination-specific assessment.
reason.Professional Accountant (SA) — SAIPA: SAIPA has limited reciprocity (IFA UK, IPA Australia) and usually needs bridging exams in destination law and tax.
reason.CIMA (CGMA): CIMA is an international designation in its own right, portable to the UK, Australia, Canada and Ireland without a SAICA agreement.
reason.ACCA: ACCA is globally portable through its own recognition network, so no SAICA agreement is needed.
reason.Degree only, no professional designation: A degree alone limits you to non-designated finance roles; a professional designation greatly widens your options.
reason.Studying / in articles: Still qualifying: completing your designation is the single biggest thing you can do for your mobility.
reason.Other: Confirm how your qualification is recognised in your target country before applying.

reason.Active member in good standing: Active membership lets you request a Letter of Good Standing and start a recognition application now.
reason.Member, fees in arrears: Clear your fees first; a Letter of Good Standing needs an account in good standing.
reason.Lapsed: Reinstate your membership before applying, as recognition routes require current standing.
reason.Not a member / not yet admitted: Without professional membership the recognition routes are closed; admission is the first step.

reason.None started: You have not begun destination recognition; request your Letter of Good Standing via my.saica.co.za to start (it is free).
reason.Researching / Letter of Good Standing re: Good start; submit to the destination body (ICAEW, CA ANZ, CPA Canada or CPA Ireland) once your letter arrives.
reason.Application submitted: Your recognition application is in progress; follow up directly with the partner body, never through a paid intermediary.
reason.Completed / admitted: You already hold destination-body membership, so the hardest credential step is behind you.

reason.No — commerce / industry roles (no audit: Commerce and industry roles only, so MRA membership covers you fully with no extra audit qualification needed.
reason.I hold RA / audit signing rights in SA: Holding SA audit signing rights is a strong base, but statutory audit signing abroad still needs the destination's own audit qualification and practising certificate.
reason.Not sure: Clarify whether you need statutory audit signing abroad; MRA membership covers finance roles but not audit rights, and the two routes differ significantly.
reason.Yes — I need public practice or audit ri: Statutory audit signing is not covered by MRA membership; it needs a separate destination audit qualification, a practising certificate and Registered Auditor status, a materially longer path.

reason.Tax: Tax specialists are in strong demand across the UK, Australia and Ireland.
reason.External Audit: Audit experience is highly portable and valued by international firms.
reason.Financial Accounting / Reporting: IFRS reporting skills transfer directly to most destinations.
reason.Management Accounting / FP&A: FP&A and commercial finance roles are in steady demand abroad.
reason.Advisory / Corporate Finance: Advisory and corporate finance experience opens senior commercial roles.
reason.Internal Audit: Internal audit is sought after in larger international organisations.
reason.Forensic: Forensic accounting is a niche but well-paid specialism abroad.
reason.Insolvency / Restructuring: Insolvency experience is valued but more jurisdiction-specific.
reason.Public Sector: Public-sector finance is more localised; private-sector roles travel more easily.
reason.Other: Confirm how your specialism maps to demand in your target market.

rules.empty_reason: No function specialism recorded. Audit, tax and IFRS reporting experience travels best, so add yours.

ranges.reason: Under 2 years post-qualifying; most employer-sponsored visas favour more experience.
2 to 5 years post-qualifying meets the bar for most overseas finance roles.
5 to 10 years is a strong, sponsor-ready experience level.
10+ years experience, a senior and highly employable profile.

reason.Trainee / in articles: Still in training; finish your articles and qualify before applying abroad.
reason.Newly qualified (0–2 years post): Newly qualified, workable but a year or two more post-qualifying experience strengthens applications.
reason.Manager / Senior: Manager-level experience is a sweet spot for sponsored overseas roles.
reason.Senior Manager / Associate Director: Senior experience opens competitive, well-paid roles abroad.
reason.Director / Partner / CFO: Executive-level profile; target senior commercial and leadership roles.

reason.Valid, 2+ years remaining: Passport valid, meets typical work-visa requirements.
reason.Valid, under 2 years remaining: Renew soon, most work visas need 2+ years remaining on your passport.
reason.No passport: No passport; apply immediately, this is the first blocker.

reason.Current (within 6 months): Current police clearance, meets visa-ready standard.
reason.Older than 6 months: Most visa applications require a clearance issued within the last 6 months; renew it.
reason.None: No police clearance; apply now, SAPS turnaround is typically 4 to 8 weeks.

reason.Under R30k: Tight: recognition fees, the apostille and relocation usually run higher, so build a buffer.
reason.R30k – R70k: Covers recognition fees and basic relocation costs but tight for a family move.
reason.R70k – R150k: Comfortable budget for recognition, visa and relocation for most destinations.
reason.R150k+: Strong financial position for relocation, including family routes.
reason.Prefer not to say: Budget not declared; share it privately with us if it shapes your options.

reason.Basic: Basic English is a real blocker for client-facing finance roles; build fluency.
reason.Conversational: Conversational level works for some roles but most employers expect fluent business English.
reason.Fluent: Fluent English meets the bar for international finance roles.
reason.Native / first language: First-language English clears the language bar for all major destinations.

reason.Not needed for my route: No test required for your route; confirm this with the destination's visa rules.
reason.Not yet: Some skilled-worker visas (UK, Australia, Canada) need an English test even for first-language speakers; check early.
reason.IELTS / PTE done: Test done, one less visa hurdle to clear.
reason.Exempt: Exemption noted; keep evidence of it for your visa application.

reason.Single, no dependants: Single, the easiest profile to relocate; no dependant visas needed.
reason.Married / partnered, no children: Partner visa adds a step but is workable.
reason.Married / partnered with children: Relocating with children adds dependant visas, schooling and cost; plan for it.
reason.Single parent with children: Single-parent dependant visa rules vary by country; get specialist guidance.
reason.Supporting elderly parents in SA: Caring for parents in SA; consider destinations with faster family-visa routes or shorter contracts.

reason.As soon as possible: ASAP timeline; start your recognition application and job search now.
reason.Within 6 months: 6-month horizon is realistic once your Letter of Good Standing and documents are ready.
reason.Within 1 year: 1-year horizon is ideal for recognition, the apostille and visa processing.
reason.Just exploring: Exploring only; no urgency means you can plan the recognition route strategically.
